projects.confirm_delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?
projects.confirm_new_message: Your current factory and program will be cleared. This cannot be undone.

level_1.name: First Part
level_1.description: Place a Fabricator, connect it to the Shipper with a belt, and remember to start both machines so the Fabricator produces parts and the Shipper receives them.

level_2.name: Assembly Line
level_2.description: Connect machines with belts and assemble your first robots.

level_3.name: Mass Production
level_3.description: Use loops to produce 10 parts efficiently.

level_4.name: Quality Matters
level_4.description: Use a Splitter and a Recycler to keep defective parts out of the Shipper.

level_5.name: Smart Routing
level_5.description: Use variables and splitters to produce multiple robot types.

level_6.name: Custom Robots
level_6.description: Define functions to build two different robot models.

level_7.name: Rush Order!
level_7.description: React to events and meet a tight deadline.

level_8.name: Optimize Everything
level_8.description: Balance speed, cost, and quality to earn maximum stars.

level_9.name: Robot Expo
level_9.description: Sandbox showcase — build anything you want!

level_10.name: Factory Tycoon
level_10.description: Run a multi-line production facility for both robot types.

level_failed.title: Level Failed
level_failed.message: You didn't meet the level objective. Try again!

reason.unconsumable_input: The {{machine}} can't use {{item}}. Check its recipe or where the belt leads.
reason.unconsumable_input_machine_disabled: The {{machine}} is stopped, so it can't accept {{item}}. Start the {{machine}} and try again.
reason.no_recipe: You started the {{machine}} without a recipe. Set a recipe before starting it.
reason.starvation: Machine {{machine}} is missing {{item}} for its recipe.

fallback.label: Factory Code (TypeScript)

editor.language_reload_notice: Language change applies to the editor after page reload.

tutorial.level1_step1: Welcome! Double-click on the grid to place your first machine — a Fabricator.
tutorial.level1_step2: The Shipper is already on the grid. Find it — it will ship the parts your Fabricator produces.
tutorial.level1_step3: Connect them with a belt — drag from the Fabricator's orange output to the Shipper's green input.
tutorial.level1_step4: Both machines aren't running yet — you'll need to program them. Open the Editor to write your program. Click the Code button or press E.
tutorial.level1_step5: Use the 'set recipe of <machine> to <recipe>' block to choose what your machine produces. Drag it from the Machines category and pick Fabricator from the <machine> dropdown.
tutorial.level1_step6: Now add two 'start <machine>' blocks — one for the Fabricator and one for the Shipper. Drag them from the Machines category and set one dropdown to Fabricator and the other to Shipper. Without them, no machine will run.
tutorial.level1_step7: Close the Editor and press Play. Your Fabricator and Shipper will start and parts will travel along the belt.
tutorial.level2_step1: This time you need multiple machines. Double-click to place a Fabricator and an Assembler.
tutorial.level2_step2: Connect machines with belts and route parts to the Shipper. Start the Shipper before you expect deliveries.
tutorial.level2_step3: Open the Editor and use 'set recipe' blocks to tell each machine what to produce.
tutorial.level3_step1: Open the Editor — you now have Loop blocks! Use 'repeat' to produce many parts efficiently.
tutorial.level3_step2: Loops repeat your instructions automatically, so you don't have to write the same blocks over and over.
tutorial.level4_step1: Defective parts ruin your Quality score! Use a Splitter and a Recycler to catch defects before they reach the Shipper.
tutorial.level4_step2: Place a Splitter on the path between your Assembler and the Shipper. Then place a Recycler off to one side, and connect a belt from the Splitter to the Recycler.
tutorial.level4_step3: Open the Editor — at level 4 a new 'route items of splitter to' action appears in the Machines category, and 'on item arrives at' appears in Events. Combine them to react each time a part enters your Splitter.
tutorial.level4_step4: Inside the handler, check 'current item is defective' (in Conditionals). If it is, use 'route items of <splitter> to Left' to send the next item to the Recycler; otherwise use 'route items of <splitter> to Forward' to send it to the Shipper. The handler must set the route on every item — the Splitter remembers its last setting.
